title: ws_backoff_idx and ws_consecutive_failures drift by 1 at failure threshold
description: client_task.rs: At the 5th consecutive WS failure, ws_consecutive_failures >= WS_MAX_FAILURES is true so ws_needs_restart is left false and reconnect_ws() is NOT called — meaning ws_backoff_idx is NOT incremented for that failure. If retry is ever re-enabled after EphemeralUnavailable (e.g. after session refresh), ws_backoff_idx will be 4 instead of 5, causing one retry with 16s backoff instead of 30s. Latent maintenance trap; harmless in current code.
close_reason: Won't fix: drift has zero effect in current code (no path re-enables WS after EphemeralUnavailable). Fixing it would add dead speculative code, violating the no-dead-code rule. Accepted known drift.
